info.title: PixlStash API
info.description: PixlStash backend API for picture management, tagging, stacks, sets, character workflows and ComfyUI integration.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's UI and behavior configuration payload.

patch.description: Applies a partial config patch for the authenticated user and returns updated settings.

get.description: Returns the smart-score penalised tags for the authenticated user. Accessible to READ-scoped tokens.
Returns authentication and session-related information for the current request.

post.description: Changes the authenticated user's password according to auth policy.

get.description: Lists personal access tokens owned by the authenticated user.

post.description: Creates a personal access token for the authenticated user.

delete.description: Deletes one personal access token by id for the authenticated user.

patch.description: Updates mutable fields on a personal access token (currently: watermark).

get.description: Returns the user's watermark as a PNG. Returns the default if no custom watermark is set.

post.description: Uploads a PNG/JPEG/WebP image to use as the user's watermark.

delete.description: Removes the user's custom watermark; the default will be used for new shares.

get.description: Returns the IDs of resources of the given type that have at least one active READ share token. Accepts ?resource_type= (character, picture_set, project, picture).

post.description: Given a list of picture IDs, returns which ones have active READ share tokens.

delete.summary: Revoke all tokens for a resource
delete.description: Deletes all READ tokens scoped to a specific resource (by type and id).

get.description: Returns the access scope for the current session or token. Accepts ?token= query parameter so unauthenticated share-link recipients can discover what the token grants before loading the UI.
Returns background worker progress plus process CPU, RAM, and VRAM usage metrics.
Returns watch-folder paths from import-folder records in the database.
Returns the configured filesystem browser root paths. When non-empty, the filesystem browser is restricted to these directories. An empty list means the browser is unrestricted.

post.description: Opens the server config path in the operating system file browser.

get.description: Returns summary counts and thumbnail reference for ALL, UNASSIGNED, SCRAPHEAP, or a specific character id.
Returns picture ids selected as reference faces for the given character.

patch.description: Updates character fields and clears dependent picture text embeddings when identity data changes.

delete.description: Deletes a character, clears character assignment from faces, and removes its reference set when present.

get.description: Returns a single character record by id.

post.description: Given a list of picture IDs, returns character_assignments (character_id → [picture_ids]) and pictures_with_faces ([picture_ids]). Used by the AddToCharacter menu to load membership in a single request.

get.summary: Get character by project name and character name
get.description: Returns a character record by name within a named project.
Returns one character field value, including generated thumbnail handling for field=thumbnail.
Lists characters, optionally filtered by exact name or project. Pass ``project_id`` as a numeric ID to restrict to one project, or ``UNASSIGNED`` for characters with no project.

post.description: Creates a character and its linked reference picture set.
Assigns provided face ids or largest faces from picture ids to a character.

delete.description: Removes character assignment from provided face ids or from faces in provided picture ids.

get.description: Returns picture sets with visible member counts, top pictures, and thumbnail URLs.

post.description: Creates a new picture set with name and optional description.

get.summary: Get picture set by project name and set name
get.description: Returns picture set metadata for a named set within a named project.

post.description: Given a list of picture IDs, returns a map of set_id → [picture_ids] for every set that contains at least one of the requested pictures. Also expands stack siblings so all stack members are considered. Used by the AddToSet menu to load membership in a single request.

get.description: Returns or generates a cached composite thumbnail representing top-scoring pictures in a set.

Returns set metadata or member pictures with optional sort, format, and character-likeness/smart-score modes. By default only the stack leader picture is returned for each stack. Pass expand_stacks=true to receive every picture in every stack.

patch.description: Updates picture set name and/or description.

delete.description: Deletes a picture set and all its membership links.

get.description: Returns unique picture ids that belong to a set, with optional deleted inclusion. By default only explicitly stored members are returned. Pass expand_stacks=true to also include all stack siblings of any member, which is useful for checking whether a stack is part of the set.

post.description: Adds a batch of pictures to a set (non-destructive). Skips pictures already in the set.

put.description: Atomically replaces the entire member list of a set. All existing members are removed and the provided picture ids become the new members.

post.description: Adds one picture to a set when the set and picture are valid and membership does not already exist.

delete.description: Removes one picture membership from a picture set.

schema.title: Response List Projects Api V1 Projects Get

post.description: Given a list of picture IDs, returns project_assignments (project_id → [picture_ids]) and unassigned_picture_ids ([picture_ids with no project membership]). Used by the AddToProject menu to load membership in a single request.

get.summary: List picture sets for a project
get.description: Returns all picture sets that belong to the given project. ``id_or_name`` may be a numeric ID or a project name (case-insensitive).

get.summary: Get a project by ID or name
get.description: Returns the project matching the given numeric ID or name (case-insensitive).

delete.description:
Delete a project.

Characters and picture sets belonging to the project have their
project_id nulled (they become unassigned).  Attachments and their
files are permanently removed.

get.description: Returns the number of pictures assigned to a project. Use 'UNASSIGNED' as project_id to count pictures with no project.
Download a ZIP archive of the project: metadata, attachment files, and optionally all pictures belonging to its characters and picture sets.

schema.title: Response List Attachments Api V1 Projects  Project Id  Attachments Get

post.summary: Upload an attachment to a project
Add a URL bookmark to a project

post.description: Adds a tag to a picture and removes empty-tag sentinel when appropriate.

get.description: Returns all tags currently attached to a picture.

delete.description: Removes all tags from a picture in a single operation and restores the empty-tag sentinel.
Removes one tag from a picture by numeric tag id and restores empty-tag sentinel when needed.

post.description: Removes a tag value from the picture and its face/hand associations for that picture.

get.description: Returns all unique tag values with their usage count, sorted by count descending then alphabetically.

post.description: Returns tags for each requested picture id. At most 200 ids accepted per call.

get.description: Returns stack metadata and ordered picture ids for a stack.
Returns ordered picture payloads for a stack using grid or metadata field sets.
Returns the stack containing a picture, or null stack information when unstacked.

post.description: Creates a new stack or reuses an existing compatible one and assigns provided pictures to it.

patch.description: Sets explicit order for all members in a stack using a complete ordered id list.

post.description: Adds pictures to an existing stack while preventing cross-stack membership conflicts.

delete.description: Removes pictures from a stack and deletes the stack when one or fewer members remain.

patch.description: Moves a single stack member to the given 0-based position, shifting all other members as needed.

get.summary: Get tag predictions for a picture
get.description: Returns all stored tag predictions for the given picture, ordered by confidence descending.  Use the ``status`` query param to filter by ``PENDING``, ``CONFIRMED``, or ``REJECTED``.

post.description: Marks the prediction as CONFIRMED and ensures a corresponding row exists in the Tag table.  Emits a CHANGED_PICTURES event.
Marks the prediction as REJECTED.  Does not modify the Tag table.

post.summary: Delete tag predictions for a picture
post.description: Deletes all TagPrediction rows for the picture except those with model_version='manual' (user-rejected tags), so the background tagger treats it as never seen and rebuilds predictions from scratch.

post.summary: Reset tags and predictions for a picture
post.description: Atomically deletes all non-manual TagPrediction rows and all Tag rows for the picture, then restores the pending-retag sentinel.  This is the single-round-trip equivalent of calling tag_predictions/delete followed by DELETE tags — it avoids the intermediate state where predictions are gone but tags still exist, which otherwise tricks the background MissingTagFinder into running a wasted inference pass.
Clears the picture's description field and queues a new description inference pass.  Pass a 'model' field in the request body to override which description plugin to use for this specific picture.

get.summary: Get per-label thresholds for the PixlStash tagger
get.description: Returns each label's base threshold and the effective threshold after applying the current user offset. Results are sorted alphabetically.

Return all scores submitted by this guest session.

Requires a READ-scoped token.  The guest_session cookie must be present
and valid (resolved by auth_middleware into request.state.guest_session_id).

Returns:
    A JSON object ``{"scores": {"<picture_id>": <score>, ...}}``.

post.description:
Submit or update star scores for one or more pictures.

This is the sole write endpoint accessible to READ-scoped tokens.

Request body (JSON):
    session_id (str): Client-generated UUID (max 64 chars, ``[A-Za-z0-9_-]``).
    set_cookie (bool): When True the server sets persistent cookies.
    scores (dict[str, int]): Mapping of picture_id → score (0-5).
        At most 500 entries per request.

Returns:
    ``{"ok": true}`` on success.

Raises:
    400: Validation error (bad session_id, bad score value, too many entries).
    503: Too many concurrent active guest sessions (new session refused).

delete.description:
Clear the guest session cookies for this browser.

Removes the ``guest_session`` and ``guest_session_active`` cookies so
the browser starts a fresh anonymous session on the next page load.
The scores stored in the database are retained (they may still be used
for aggregate statistics); we simply sever the link between this browser
and those scores.

Requires a READ-scoped token.

Returns:
    ``{"ok": true}``

get.description: Returns all available sorting keys and direction semantics supported by picture listing and search endpoints.
Lists available image plugins and their parameter schemas.

post.description: Runs a named image plugin on selected pictures and imports outputs into stacks.

get.description: Builds groups from likeness edges using filtering options such as character, set, format, and threshold.

post.description: Opens the containing folder of a reference picture in the OS file manager.

get.description: Returns tag statistics for the current view filter, cached for 60 seconds.
Returns a WebP thumbnail for a picture id, generating and caching it on demand when needed.

post.description: Returns thumbnail URLs and mapped face/hand overlays for a list of picture ids, including penalised-tag hints.

get.description: Queues an asynchronous export task and returns a task id for polling status and downloading the generated archive.
Returns current progress for an export task id, including completion state and download URL when ready.
Downloads the generated export file for a completed task id.
Performs semantic text search across pictures with optional sort, filtering, and candidate scoping.

post.description: Starts an asynchronous import of uploaded image/video files (or zip contents) and returns a task id.

get.description: Returns progress and result information for a previously started import task.

patch.description: Assigns, removes, or clears project association for a batch of pictures.

post.description: Applies 0-5 manual scores to multiple pictures in one request while optionally enforcing only-unscored updates.

get.description: Streams the original media file for a picture id when the requested extension matches the stored format.
Returns metadata, tags, and optional smart score for a single picture, including embedded file metadata when available.

post.description: Adds a face bounding box to a picture and frame index, updating sentinel/ordering behavior for manual annotations.

delete.description: Deletes a face at frame 0 by index and reindexes remaining faces for stable ordering.

get.description: Computes max character-likeness score for faces in a picture against a reference character.
Returns a single picture field value; large binary fields are base64 encoded and thumbnail returns image bytes.

patch.description: Updates mutable picture fields from query/body parameters, including tag replacement when provided.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a picture by marking it deleted, making it appear in scrapheap views.

post.description: Restores deleted pictures from scrapheap, either all deleted pictures or a provided picture id subset.

delete.description: Permanently removes deleted pictures from database and disk for provided ids or for all scrapheap items when omitted.

get.description: Lists pictures with filtering, sort, pagination, and optional grid field projection.

parameters.name: only_deleted

schema.description: Return only deleted (scrapheap) pictures. Replaces the deprecated character_id=SCRAPHEAP.
schema.title: Only Deleted

parameters.description: Return only deleted (scrapheap) pictures. Replaces the deprecated character_id=SCRAPHEAP.

get.description: Fetches a single batch of pictures and reports completion via an explicit `done` flag derived from the underlying SQL row count (not the post-filter row count). Callers paginate by passing the returned `next_offset` until `done` is true.

parameters.name: only_deleted

schema.description: Return only deleted (scrapheap) pictures. Replaces the deprecated character_id=SCRAPHEAP.
schema.title: Only Deleted

parameters.description: Return only deleted (scrapheap) pictures. Replaces the deprecated character_id=SCRAPHEAP.

get.summary: Total picture count for a listing filter
get.description: Returns the total number of pictures matching the same filter set used by `/pictures` and `/pictures/stream`.

parameters.name: only_deleted

schema.description: Return only deleted (scrapheap) pictures. Replaces the deprecated character_id=SCRAPHEAP.
schema.title: Only Deleted

parameters.description: Return only deleted (scrapheap) pictures. Replaces the deprecated character_id=SCRAPHEAP.

get.description: Lists discovered built-in and user workflows with placeholder validation metadata.

delete.description: Deletes a workflow JSON from the user workflow directory.

post.description: Interrupts the currently running ComfyUI prompt and clears the pending queue.
Submits i2i prompts for one or more picture ids and imports generated outputs back into PixlStash.
Submits a t2i prompt using only a caption and imports generated outputs back into PixlStash.
Saves a workflow JSON into the user workflow directory, optionally overwriting an existing file.

get.summary: Get ComfyUI workflow for a picture
get.description: Extracts and returns the ComfyUI workflow embedded in a picture's file metadata, if present.
Returns all reference folders and metadata about the current runtime mode.

post.description: Adds a new reference folder. The path must be absolute and must not point to a restricted system directory. In Docker mode the folder is created with status 'pending_mount' and requires a server restart to mount-verify the path. Outside Docker the path is verified immediately and the folder becomes 'active' (or 'mount_error') right away.

patch.description: Updates editable fields for a reference folder.

delete.description: Removes a reference folder record. Pictures indexed from this folder are de-associated but not deleted from the database or disk.

post.description: Gracefully terminates the server process. The process manager (systemd, Docker, etc.) is expected to restart it automatically. Use this after adding reference folders to apply pending mount changes.

post.summary: Open reference folder in file manager
post.description: Opens the reference folder (or an optional subdirectory within it) in the OS file manager.

get.description: Returns all folders used by the automatic import watcher.

post.description: Adds a folder watched for automatic imports.

patch.description: Updates label and import behavior for an import folder.

delete.description: Removes an import folder from automatic monitoring.

get.description: Returns immediate child entries of the requested directory. Available in native (non-Docker) installs only. Requires authentication.

parameters.name: path

schema.description: Absolute directory path to list. Defaults to home directory.
schema.title: Path

parameters.description: Absolute directory path to list. Defaults to home directory.

get.summary: List tagger plugins and current settings
get.description:
Return every registered tagger/captioner plugin together with
the current user's ``tagger_settings``.

Response shape::

    {
      "plugins": [
        {
          "name": "pixlstash_tagger",
          "display_name": "PixlStash Tagger",
          "description": "...",
          "supports_tags": true,
          "supports_descriptions": false,
          "requires_download": true,
          "default_enabled": true,
          "parameter_schema": [...],
          "downloaded_artifacts": [],
          "is_loaded": false,
          "load_error": null
        },
        ...
      ],
      "settings": { ... }
    }

post.summary: Start artifact download for a tagger plugin
post.description:
Kick off a background download for the named plugin.

Returns immediately with ``{"status": "started"}`` or
``{"status": "not_required"}`` when no download is needed.
Download progress is logged to the server console.

Raises 404 if the plugin is not registered.

delete.summary: Delete a downloaded artifact for a tagger plugin
delete.description:
Remove a downloaded artifact and unload the plugin if currently loaded.

Raises 404 if the plugin or artifact is not found.

new_password.title: New Password
new_password.description: Password must be at least 8 characters long

password.title: Password
password.description: Password must be at least 8 characters long

ResetTagsRequest.title: ResetTagsRequest
ResetTagsRequest.description: Request body for reset_tags and reset_description endpoints.

tags.name: config
tags.description: User configuration, auth helpers, worker progress and server config utilities.

tags.description: Character CRUD, summaries, reference pictures and face assignment endpoints.
